## What's Changed

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## New Features 🎉

* **Recurring reminders** for the default reminder format (#319)
* **Do-not-disturb mode** for reminder notifications (#318)
* Option to show the reminder popup together with the system notification (#317)
* Non-task lines are now converted into tasks when inserting a reminder (#314)
* New setting to exclude specific files/folders from reminder scanning (#313)
* The Tasks plugin due date now supports an optional time part (#312)
* The date/time chooser now appears inline near the cursor in Live Preview (#307)
* New settings to disable notifications and to open the note by clicking a notification (#306)
* `+ [ ]` is now recognized as a valid task format (#260) — thanks @ljbkusters!

## Bug Fixes 🐛

* The `Show Reminders` command now reveals the reminder list even when the sidebar is collapsed (#253, fixed in #303)
* Picking a date on a non-task line now shows an explanatory notice instead of silently doing nothing (#176, #190, fixed in #303)
* Kanban plugin's "link date to daily note" dates are now recognized without restarting Obsidian (#30, fixed in #303)
* Non-ASCII tags are now fully removed from reminder titles (#169, fixed in #303)
* Tags are no longer lost when editing dates in the Tasks plugin format (#311)

## Documentation 📚

* Documented the week start setting (#245) and the convert-non-task-lines setting (#316)
* Caught the docs site up with recently added settings and behaviors (#315)
* Docs site cleanups (#304)

<details>
<summary>Internal Changes & Dependency Updates 🔧</summary>

### Development / CI

* chore: modernize CI toolchain (Node 22, actions v4, lockfile v3) (#272)
* chore: align obsidian/electron/@types/node with current platform versions (#282)
* Modernize local development flow with esbuild watch and hot-reload (#285)
* chore: update devDependencies within current majors (#284)
* chore: migrate to Jest 30 (#287)
* chore: migrate to ESLint 10 and update dev tooling majors (#288)
* chore: migrate to Svelte 5 (#289)
* chore: ignore dependabot updates incompatible with current toolchain (#290)
* chore: drop obsolete prettier-plugin-svelte ignore rule (#296)
* chore: ignore eslint 10 until eslint-plugin-import supports it (#298)
* chore: ignore @types/node majors beyond the toolchain's Node version (#299)
* Maintainability improvements: enforce CI checks, fix latent bugs, reduce coupling (#300)
* ci: auto-close PRs left awaiting author response (#301)
* Add manual-verify skill and post-coding verification reminder hook (#308)
* ci: overhaul release flow (#309)
* Enable type-aware ESLint rules for floating promises and unused vars (#310)

### Dependency updates (dependabot)

* chore(deps): bump actions/checkout from 4.3.1 to 7.0.0 (#276)
* chore(deps): bump actions/cache from 4.3.0 to 6.1.0 (#274)
* chore(deps): bump actions/setup-node from 4.4.0 to 6.4.0 (#273)
* chore(deps): bump softprops/action-gh-release from 2.6.2 to 3.0.1 (#275)
* chore(deps-dev): bump eslint-plugin-svelte from 2.46.1 to 3.20.0 (#281)
* chore(deps-dev): bump cross-env from 7.0.3 to 10.1.0 (#278)
* chore(deps-dev): bump globals from 15.15.0 to 17.7.0 (#280)

</details>

## New Contributors

* @dependabot[bot] made their first contribution in #276
* @ljbkusters made their first contribution in #260

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/uphy/obsidian-reminder/compare/1.1.21...1.2.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at 1.1.21 -->

## What's Changed
### Other Changes
* Added support for week start configuration by @Raulvalverdeleal in https://github.com/uphy/obsidian-reminder/pull/238
* Prevent UI text overflow by @uphy in https://github.com/uphy/obsidian-reminder/pull/244

## New Contributors
* @Raulvalverdeleal made their first contribution in https://github.com/uphy/obsidian-reminder/pull/238

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/uphy/obsidian-reminder/compare/1.1.20...1.1.21

---

## Overview

A minor release focusing on calendar configurability, UI accessibility, and text wrapping improvements, along with a small internal fix to weekend calculation and engine metadata.

## Changes

### Features

- Calendar week-start configuration
  - New setting “Week start” allowing users to choose the first day of the week.
  - Calendar rendering, navigation, and printed calendar string now respect the configured starting weekday.
  - UI column headers in the calendar dynamically reflect the chosen start day.

### Fixes

- Correct weekend/holiday detection
  - Day.isHoliday now computes weekend days based on the configured locale first day-of-week instead of hardcoding Sunday/Saturday.
- UI text overflow and accessibility
  - Prevent reminder text overflow via improved wrapping (word-break: break-word; text-wrap: pretty) in IconText.svelte.
  - Improve screen reader support by adding aria-labels to the reminder title and file button.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at 1.1.20 -->

## What's Changed
### Other Changes
* Introduce the settings for the reminder list date format by @uphy in https://github.com/uphy/obsidian-reminder/pull/230


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/uphy/obsidian-reminder/compare/1.1.19...1.1.20

---

Added configurable date/time display formats for Reminder List (addresses inconsistent sidebar formatting)

This change introduces user-configurable display formats for dates and times shown in the Reminder List and related UI, resolving the inconsistency where the sidebar was fixed to M/DD or MM/DD regardless of the user’s preferred note date format.

What’s included
- New settings in the plugin Settings → Date/Time Display Format:
  - Year & Month Format (default: YYYY, MMMM)
  - Month & Day Format (default: MM/DD)
  - Short Date with Weekday Format (default: M/DD (ddd))
  - Time Format (default: HH:mm)
  Each accepts Moment-style format strings.

- Command Updated: Set date display format
  - Opens a preset chooser with live examples.
  - Presets include:
    - US Style (12h): MMMM YYYY, MM/DD, M/DD (ddd), h:mm A
    - US Style (24h): MMMM YYYY, MM/DD, M/DD (ddd), HH:mm
    - EU Style (24h): MMMM YYYY, DD/MM, D/MM (ddd), HH:mm
    - EU Style (12h): MMMM YYYY, DD/MM, D/MM (ddd), h:mm A
    - JP Style (24h): YYYY年MM月, MM/DD, M月D日 (ddd), HH:mm
    - JP Style (12h): YYYY年MM月, MM/DD, M月D日 (ddd), h:mm A
  - Selecting a preset immediately saves the formats and refreshes the Reminder List.

## How to configure

### Option A: Use presets

1. Open Command Palette.
2. Run “Set date display format”.
3. Choose a preset that matches your locale preference. The list shows live examples for the current date and time.

### Option B: Set custom formats

1. Settings → Date/Time Display Format.
2. Provide Moment-style strings for each of:
   - Year & Month (e.g., YYYY, MMMM; or YYYY年MM月)
   - Month & Day (e.g., DD. MM.; or DD/MM)
   - Short Date with Weekday (e.g., D. M. ddd)
   - Time (e.g., HH:mm; or h:mm A)
3. The Reminder List updates immediately after changes.

### Notes and compatibility

- This change is display-only and does not alter parsing or note formats.
- Defaults maintain the prior behavior (e.g., Month & Day default MM/DD) while allowing full customization to DD. MM., DD/MM, etc.
- Presets are provided as convenience; you can supply any valid Moment format.
- Applies to Windows, macOS, and mobile platforms; the live UI refresh is automatic after settings change.

releases.author: uphy

releases.author: uphy
releases.description:
## ✨ New features

- Introduce custom emoji(⏰) to Tasks Plugin format #17
- Add feature to link dates to daily notes #22
- <kbd>Meta</kbd>(<kbd>Command</kbd> or <kbd>Control</kbd>) + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>Enter</kbd> to toggle TODO items.
    - In case of Tasks Plugin format, done date is updated.  Also recurring date is processed(#17).
- Show calendar popup by shortcut(<kbd>Meta</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>2</kbd>)
- Overdue reminders in reminder list view #18
    - When you type `Esc` key in the reminder modal or click the out of reminder modal, you can mute the reminder.
    - If you mute reminder, the reminder won't show again until you restart Obsidian.
    - Muted reminders are appeared in reminder list view as `Overdue` reminders.
    - When you click the overdue reminder in the list, the reminder modal shows again.
- Click file path of reminder modal to open the reminder file #18

## 👍 Improvements

- Introduce validation in setting screen

## 🐛 Bug fix

- Kanban plugin format doesn't work if user changes its setting
